source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → autonomous-loop (modeled as an externally-driven loop; cf. claudecode sibling)
notes: Codex has no built-in autonomous self-prompting mode (no /goal, no full-auto). The loop is driven EXTERNALLY: the driver feeds successive prompts to one live codex REPL session, each prompt advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal (count 1→2→3). The spec asserts what the irrlicht autonomous-loop primitive actually cares about for a turn-delimited adapter: each iteration cycles ready→working→ready cleanly on the SAME rollout session_id, the session never sticks in working and never flips to waiting (codex has no permission gates), and the loop ends in ready. This mirrors the claudecode sibling, which also models the loop as per-iteration working→ready arcs rather than the spec's strict 'stays continuously working' span. Each iter's _done anchors to its own _start (not to session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-iteration ready, not an earlier handoff.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Loop start: first externally-fed prompt → ready → working on the rollout session
First iteration completes → working → ready (state cycles cleanly between iterations, not stuck working)
Next loop iteration: harness re-prompts → ready → working on the same session — loop is active
Second iteration completes → back to ready
Final loop iteration: harness re-prompts → ready → working on the same session
Loop terminates: final iteration ends in ready; the session never entered waiting across the whole loop (no permission gates)
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
